channel.title: Quiet Pages
channel.description: An independent magazine on writing, design, and the slow web. Published occasionally, read closely.

item.title: The quiet craft of writing on the web
item.dc:creator: Elena March
item.description: Notes on publishing slowly in a fast medium, and why the open web still rewards patience and care.

item.title: In praise of the narrow column
item.dc:creator: Samuel Okafor
item.description: Why measure, that simple, ancient typographic constraint, is still the single most important decision on a reading page.

item.title: Field notes from a small harbour
item.dc:creator: Mira Iwasaki
item.description: A week of walking, reading, and watching the boats come in. What slow places teach about attention.

item.title: Building tools that disappear
item.dc:creator: Samuel Okafor
item.description: The best software for thinking gets out of the way. A short meditation on restraint as an engineering value.

item.title: A conversation with a bookbinder
item.dc:creator: Mira Iwasaki
item.description: On hand-stitched signatures, the smell of linen thread, and what a centuries-old craft still has to teach the rest of us.

item.title: Writing against the algorithm
item.dc:creator: Elena March
item.description: A short argument for publishing in places that don't optimise for you, and for readers who arrive on purpose.

item.title: The grammar of good buttons
item.dc:creator: Samuel Okafor
item.description: A button is a sentence. A short one, in the imperative. Here is how to write it well.

item.title: What I learned from a year of walking
item.dc:creator: Mira Iwasaki
item.description: Three hundred and sixty-five days, one slow route, and a small notebook. A report from the pavement.
